system_prompt:
You are a software system decomposition agent. Your task is to decompose a function block into smaller child function blocks.

CRITICAL RULES - ENFORCED:
1. EVERY CHILD MUST BE A FUNCTION, NOT A CLASS. Never generate class definitions for child blocks.
2. Each child must have explicit: name, purpose, inputs, outputs, and boundary
3. Preserve the parent's external interface - children's composition must match parent's inputs/outputs
4. TREE STRUCTURE (not graph): The decomposition forms a tree, not a graph. Children MUST NOT call each other (no cross-calls between siblings). The parent MUST explicitly and directly invoke all its children.
5. Do NOT add extra external inputs or outputs beyond what the parent has
6. Children should be at the same abstraction level and minimally overlapping

YOU MUST DECOMPOSE. You are NOT allowed to return 0 children or refuse to decompose. Even if the node seems simple, you MUST break it into at least 2 child functions.

OUTPUT FORMAT - You MUST return valid JSON with this exact structure:
{
  "children": [
    {
      "name": "ChildName",
      "purpose": "Clear description of what this child function does",
      "inputs": [{"name": "param", "type": "str", "description": "desc", "source": "where data comes from"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "result", "type": "int", "description": "desc", "consumer": "who uses this output"}],
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["..."], "out_of_scope": ["..."]},
      "behavior": "Detailed description of expected behavior - how this function transforms inputs to outputs",
      "signature": "def ChildName(param1: type1, param2: type2) -> return_type",
      "stop_decompose": false,
      "stop_reason": "",
      "global_vars": [
        {"variable": "data_store_name", "op": "read|write|read_write", "description": "what operation is needed"}
      ]
    }
  ],
  "decomposition_rationale": "Explain HOW these children work together to implement the parent."
}
user_prompt:
Decompose the following function block:

Node Name: Project Task Manager
Node Purpose: Project Task Manager

SubPRD Context:
  Task Description:
    Inputs: input: Any

    Functional Requirements:
    [FR-001] Create Project: Create a new project with name, description, and owner. Check that owner exists. Default status 'planning'. Record creation time.
    [FR-002] Update Project: Update project info: description, owner, status, deadline. Check project exists.
    [FR-003] Delete Project: Delete project and all its tasks. Check project exists.
    [FR-004] Create Task: Create a task under a project with title, description, priority, estimated hours. Check project exists and status is planning or active. Default status 'todo'. No assignee.
    [FR-005] Assign Task: Assign a task to a team member. Check task exists and unassigned or reassignable. Check member exists and available. Optionally check skills. Update assignee_id. Set member status to busy.
    [FR-006] Update Task Status: Update task status. Check task exists. Status flow: todo->in_progress->review->done. Blocked can be set anytime. Record actual hours when done.
    [FR-007] Complete Task: Complete a task. Check status is 'review'. Update status to 'done'. Record actual hours. Release member (set available).
    [FR-008] Delete Task: Delete a task. Check task exists. If assigned, release member.
    [FR-009] List Project Tasks: List tasks of a project with optional filters: status, priority, assignee. Show task details and progress.
    [FR-010] Get Member Tasks: Get all tasks for a member, current and historical. Calculate total hours.
    [FR-011] Get Project Progress: Get project progress: total tasks, completion rate, status counts, estimated vs actual hours, estimated completion time.
    [FR-012] Add Member: Add a team member with ID, name, role, skills, availability. Check member ID is unique.
    [FR-013] Update Member Availability: Update member availability. Check that member has no ongoing task before setting offline.

  Constraints:
    - NFR-001: Data must be consistent across the three in-memory data sources (members, projects, tasks) during operations like assignment, status updates, and deletions.
    - NFR-002: Error handling must cover invalid project/task/member, status conflicts, and state violations with appropriate error messages.

  Acceptance Criteria:
    - AC-001: Create project: owner must exist; project_id auto-generated; status defaults to 'planning'; creation time recorded.
    - AC-002: Update project: only existing projects can be updated; owner assignment checks existence; status changes are recorded.
    - AC-003: Delete project: project must exist; all associated tasks are deleted; if tasks were assigned, members are released.
    - AC-004: Create task: project must exist and be in 'planning' or 'active' status; task defaults to 'todo' and no assignee.
    - AC-005: Assign task: task must exist and be unassigned; member must exist and be 'available'; skill match is optional; assignee_id set; member status becomes 'busy'.
    - AC-006: Update task status: task must exist; status transitions follow the prescribed flow (todo->in_progress->review->done); blocked can be set anytime; actual hours recorded on done.
    - AC-007: Complete task: task must be in 'review' status; status changes to 'done'; actual hours recorded; assignee's availability set to 'available'.
    - AC-008: Delete task: task must exist; if assigned, member is released.
    - AC-009: List project tasks: returns tasks filtered by status, priority, assignee if provided; shows task details.
    - AC-010: Get member tasks: returns current and completed tasks for member; total hours computed correctly.
    - AC-011: Get project progress: returns total tasks, completion rate (completed/total), status counts, estimated vs actual hours, and estimated completion time.
    - AC-012: Add member: new member must have unique member_id; fields as specified.
    - AC-013: Update member availability: member must exist; cannot set offline if member has ongoing tasks.

Inputs:
  - input: Any - System input
Outputs:
  - output: Any - System output

Global Variables (MUST be DISTRIBUTED to children):
  - read_write on members: Stores team member information. Pre-populated with 6 members.
  - read_write on projects: Stores project information. Initially empty.
  - read_write on tasks: Stores task records. Initially empty.

  >>> Each child must declare a SUBSET of these global_vars in the 'global_vars' field. <<<
  >>> Children perform actual data operations; parent orchestrates by calling children. <<<

Maximum children allowed: 10
Maximum depth: 3
Return ONLY the JSON response.

children.name: CreateProject
children.purpose: Create a new project with name, description, and owner. Checks that owner exists. Default status 'planning'. Records creation time.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Dictionary with keys: name, description, owner_id

outputs.name: new_project
outputs.description: Created project record with id, name, description, owner_id, status, created_at
outputs.consumer: parent for response and to update global projects

children.behavior: Receives project_data and accesses global members to verify owner exists. If not, raises an error. Otherwise, generates a unique project_id (e.g., incrementing integer), sets status='planning', records created_at=current time. Returns the new project record. The parent will append it to the projects list.
children.signature: def CreateProject(project_data: dict, members: list, projects: list) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read to get next ID, write to append new project (but writing happens in parent after call? Actually child should not modify global directly, it returns new project. Parent will append. So child only reads projects to compute ID. But we mark as read because it reads to get max ID? Typically child should not mutate globals, but it can return the new record. So op=read. Parent will write.

children.name: UpdateProject
children.purpose: Update an existing project's fields: description, owner, status, deadline. Checks project exists.

inputs.name: update_data
inputs.description: Dictionary with project_id and fields to update

inputs.name: members
inputs.description: Global list of member records (to verify new owner if provided)

outputs.name: updated_project
outputs.consumer: parent to update in global projects

in_scope: Validating new owner existence if owner updated

children.behavior: Finds project by project_id in projects list. If not found, raise error. If owner_id is being updated, check that new owner exists in members. Update provided fields. Return updated project. Parent will replace in projects list.
children.signature: def UpdateProject(update_data: dict, projects: list, members: list) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read to find project, write to update (but actual write by parent)
Check new owner existence if needed

children.name: DeleteProject
children.purpose: Delete a project and all its tasks. Releases members assigned to any tasks. Checks project exists.

outputs.name: deleted_project_id
outputs.consumer: parent to confirm deletion and update globals

outputs.name: released_member_ids
outputs.description: List of member IDs that were released due to task deletion

children.behavior: Finds project by project_id. If not found, raise error. Collects all tasks with that project_id. For each task, if assigned, set that member's status to 'available'. Remove all those tasks from tasks list (or return list to parent for deletion). Remove project from projects list. Returns project_id and list of released member IDs. Parent performs actual deletions and updates.
children.signature: def DeleteProject(project_id: int, projects: list, tasks: list, members: list) -> tuple

children.name: CreateTask
children.purpose: Create a task under a project with title, description, priority, estimated hours. Checks project exists and status is 'planning' or 'active'. Default status 'todo' and no assignee.

inputs.name: task_data
inputs.description: Dictionary with project_id, title, description, priority, estimated_hours

outputs.name: new_task
outputs.description: Created task record with id, project_id, title, description, priority, estimated_hours, status='todo', assignee_id=None

children.behavior: Finds project by project_id. Checks project exists and status is 'planning' or 'active'. If not, raise error. Generate unique task_id. Create task record and return. Parent appends to tasks list.
children.signature: def CreateTask(task_data: dict, projects: list, tasks: list) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read to generate task ID (e.g., max existing id)

children.name: AssignTask
children.purpose: Assign a task to a team member. Checks task exists and unassigned or reassignable. Checks member exists and available. Optionally checks skills. Updates assignee_id. Sets member status to busy.

inputs.name: assign_data
inputs.description: Dictionary with task_id, member_id, optional skill_check

outputs.name: updated_member
outputs.description: Member record with status changed to 'busy'

in_scope: Validating task existence and assignment state

children.behavior: Finds task by task_id. If not found, raise error. If task already assigned and not reassignable (depends on policy, assume all tasks can be reassigned? but check if unassigned or reassignable - let's accept if unassigned or if reassignable, but for simplicity we allow reassignment if task is not in 'done' status). Check member exists and status is 'available'. If skill_check provided, verify member has required skills (optional). Set task.assignee_id = member_id. Set member.status = 'busy'. Return both updated task and member.
children.signature: def AssignTask(assign_data: dict, tasks: list, members: list) -> tuple

global_vars.description: Read member, update status to busy

children.name: UpdateTaskStatus
children.purpose: Update task status according to flow: todo->in_progress->review->done; blocked can be set anytime. Records actual hours when status becomes 'done'. Checks task exists.

inputs.name: status_data
inputs.description: Dictionary with task_id, new_status, optional actual_hours (if new_status='done')

outputs.name: updated_task
outputs.description: Task record with updated status and optionally actual_hours

out_of_scope: Releasing member (done in CompleteTask or Delete?)

children.behavior: Finds task by task_id. If not found, raise error. Validates transition: if new_status is 'blocked', allowed any time; else must follow todo->in_progress->review->done. If new_status is 'done', require actual_hours and record them. Update task status and return updated task.
children.signature: def UpdateTaskStatus(status_data: dict, tasks: list) -> dict

children.name: CompleteTask
children.purpose: Complete a task that is in 'review' status. Updates status to 'done', records actual hours, releases the assigned member (sets member available).

outputs.name: updated_task
outputs.description: Task record with status='done' and actual_hours

outputs.name: updated_member
outputs.description: Member record with status='available' if task was assigned

in_scope: Validating task exists and status is 'review'

children.behavior: Finds task by task_id. If not found or status != 'review', raise error. Set task.status='done', task.actual_hours=actual_hours. If task.assignee_id is set, find that member and set status='available'. Return updated task and member.
children.signature: def CompleteTask(complete_data: dict, tasks: list, members: list) -> tuple

global_vars.description: Read and update member if assigned

children.name: DeleteTask
children.purpose: Delete a task. Checks task exists. If assigned, release the member (set available).

outputs.name: released_member_id
outputs.description: Member ID that was released, if any

children.behavior: Finds task by task_id. If not found, raise error. If task.assignee_id is not None, find that member and set status='available'. Return task_id and member_id (or None). Parent removes task from list and updates member.
children.signature: def DeleteTask(task_id: int, tasks: list, members: list) -> tuple

children.name: QueryProjectInfo
children.purpose: Handle project-related queries: list project tasks with optional filters, get member tasks, get project progress. Returns structured data based on query type.

inputs.name: query_data
inputs.description: Dictionary with query_type (list_tasks, member_tasks, project_progress) and query parameters (project_id, status, priority, assignee, member_id, etc.)

outputs.name: query_result
outputs.description: Result depending on query: for list_tasks returns list of task dicts; for member_tasks returns dict with tasks and total_hours; for project_progress returns dict with progress stats

in_scope: Filtering and aggregating data based on query type

children.behavior: Parses query_data to determine query_type. For 'list_tasks': filter tasks by project_id and optional status/priority/assignee, return list. For 'member_tasks': filter tasks by assignee_id (member_id), separate current (status not done) and historical (done), calculate total_hours (sum of actual_hours or estimated? Use actual if available else estimated). For 'project_progress': check project exists, count total tasks, completed tasks (status done), compute completion rate, count by status, sum estimated_hours vs sum actual_hours (only for done tasks?), estimate completion time (e.g., based on remaining estimated hours and average rate). Return appropriate result.
children.signature: def QueryProjectInfo(query_data: dict, tasks: list, projects: list, members: list) -> any

global_vars.description: Read project info for some queries
Read member info for member tasks

children.name: ManageMember
children.purpose: Handle member management: add a new member (unique ID) and update member availability (with check for ongoing tasks).

inputs.name: member_data
inputs.description: Dictionary with action (add_member or update_availability) and relevant fields (id, name, role, skills, availability for add; member_id, new_availability for update)

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: For add: new member record; for update: updated member record

in_scope: Checking no ongoing tasks before setting offline

children.behavior: If action='add_member': check member_id is unique in members list, if duplicate raise error; create and return new member dict. If action='update_availability': find member by member_id, if not found raise error; if new_availability='offline', check that member has no tasks in progress (status not in ['done','blocked']? actually 'ongoing' means status in ['todo','in_progress','review']? The constraint says 'no ongoing task before setting offline', so check any task assigned to this member with status not in ['done','blocked']. If has ongoing, raise error; update member.availability and return updated member.
children.signature: def ManageMember(member_data: dict, members: list, tasks: list) -> dict

global_vars.description: Check for ongoing tasks when setting offline

response.decomposition_rationale: The Project Task Manager is decomposed into 10 child functions, each handling a distinct set of operations: CreateProject, UpdateProject, DeleteProject for project lifecycle; CreateTask, AssignTask, UpdateTaskStatus, CompleteTask, DeleteTask for task lifecycle; QueryProjectInfo for read-only queries (list tasks, member tasks, progress); and ManageMember for member management. This grouping respects the system boundaries and data consistency requirements, ensuring each child has a clear responsibility and accesses only necessary global data stores. The parent orchestrates by parsing input commands and delegating to the appropriate child, then updating global stores accordingly.
